justification: Meets all pass criteria: 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1, AC-2), already-shipped/delivered edge case (AC-3, AC-4), already-cancelled edge case (AC-5), plus authorization (AC-6), audit trail (AC-7), and UI scenarios. Multiple ambiguities surfaced in confidence.low_confidence_areas (5 items) and 3 detailed escalations covering cancellation window states, support-assisted scope, and refund policy. confidence.level is 55, well under 65 threshold. Business rules are clearly marked with invariant flags and assumptions are explicitly labeled as such rather than presented as facts.

observations.text: 10 acceptance criteria far exceeds the minimum 3, covering happy path, edge cases, authorization, audit, and UI states
confidence.level of 55 appropriately reflects the underspecified input
5 low_confidence_areas and 5 explicit assumptions clearly separate known from inferred
3 escalations with options and recommendations give stakeholders actionable decision points
Intermediate warehouse states (Picking/Packing) explicitly called out as ambiguous in both edge_cases and escalations
Who can cancel is addressed in BR-1 and AC-6 — authorization is not silently assumed
Non-invariant rules (BR-4, BR-5, BR-6) are clearly marked as policy decisions, not facts
BR-4 presents 'Placed or Confirmed = full refund, no fee' as a concrete rule rather than purely flagging it as assumption, though it is marked invariant:false which mitigates this
Some invented detail around cancellation fees and split payments goes beyond what the spec provides, though these are appropriately flagged in low_confidence_areas
